source.definition: to strike repeatedly with the hand or an object on a surface

explanation.sourceText: The verb "beat" originates from Old English "beatan", meaning to strike or hit. It is related to sounds produced by striking or hitting.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „beat“ provine din engleza veche „beatan“, care înseamnă a lovi sau a bate. Este legat de sunetele produse prin lovire.

source.additional: The word describes both physical actions and communicative gestures, such as knocking on a door or beating on a table.

target.definition: a lovi repetat cu mâna sau cu un obiect pe o suprafață

explanation.sourceText: The word „bate“ comes from the Latin „battĕre“, meaning "to hit". It has expanded in Romanian to describe various hitting actions.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „bate“ provine din latină „battĕre“ („a lovi“) și s-a extins pentru a descrie diverse acțiuni de lovire.

target.additional: „A bate“ can be used in many contexts, from physical actions to metaphorical expressions like "beating a new path."

explanation.sourceText: "A bate" can be used to knock as a form of greeting.
explanation.targetText: „A bate“ poate fi folosit pentru a bate la salut.

explanation.sourceText: "A bate" can also mean to strike loudly.
explanation.targetText: „A bate“ poate însemna și „a lovi tare“.

examples.sourceText: He knocks loudly on the door.

explanation.sourceText: "A bate" can also mean a gentle touch.
explanation.targetText: „A bate“ poate însemna și o atingere ușoară.

examples.sourceText: He gently tapped on the shoulder.
examples.targetText: El a bătut ușor pe umăr.

examples.sourceText: Do you knock as a greeting?
Will you knock on the door?
He was knocking on the window.
They will knock on the door.

learningTips.text: Remember "beat" as the sound of a drum beating—just like "a bate" means to hit repeatedly.
Think of the phrase "beat the door" to remember "a bate" means to knock or hit.
The English word "beat" sounds like "bate" in Romanian, both meaning to strike or hit.
Imagine someone beating a rhythm on a table to recall the action "a bate".
